primaryGoal: Reduce public API detail response transfer cost without changing target realism, pageSize, caching behavior, or DTO semantics.
notChanged: No public Work/Blog DTO semantic change except HTTP transfer encoding
No DB query shape, index, pool, or EF projection change

expectedBackendTestImpact.clientP95P99: Lower for large gzip-enabled public detail responses when transfer/receive time is material.
expectedBackendTestImpact.httpReceiving: Lower for compressible large JSON payloads.
expectedBackendTestImpact.dataReceived: Lower compressed wire bytes in gzip-capable clients.
expectedBackendTestImpact.cpuGc: Possible CPU increase under high RPS; fastest gzip selected to limit this risk.

validations.name: RED focused integration test
validations.result: failed before implementation because Content-Encoding gzip was absent

validations.name: full backend solution
validations.result: passed: Contract 1 skipped, Component 124, Unit 59, Architecture 35, Integration 229

validations.name: dev compose rebuild
validations.command: BACKEND_PUBLISH_PORT=18080 docker compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d --build backend nginx

validations.name: curl smoke
validations.result: plain 1195 B vs gzip 850 B for /api/public/works/seeded-work; gzip response included Content-Encoding and Vary

validations.name: k6 compression smoke
validations.result: 500 requests, check rate 1.0, HTTP failure rate 0, p95 2.415107 ms, http_req_receiving p95 0.170821 ms

risks: Gzip spends backend CPU on the 2 CPU shared production host.
Local seeded-work smoke proves behavior, not heavy real detail A/B improvement.
Current byte observability may not fully represent compressed wire bytes when Content-Length is absent.
This does not fix DB pressure, app elapsed spikes, ThreadPool queue spikes, or 5xx under high max VU pressure.
recommendation: Merge if CI is green, then run production-like A/B on real heavy Work/Study detail URLs over HTTPS comparing p95/p99, receiving time, data_received, CPU/GC, app elapsed, DB pressure, and 5xx.
